summary: St. Anthony's fire describes ergotism from eating ergot-contaminated grain causing gangrenous lesions mimicking a fiery burn; other toxins like aflatoxin are excluded due to different presentations. The hallmark feature matches ergot alkaloid toxicity specifically. Agent1 reasoned based primarily on defining characteristics of ergot-induced conditions versus alternatives provided. Key was recognizing unique presentation involving tissue damage resembling "burning". Summary captures essence without extraneous details while focusing directly on critical distinguishing features between options presented for this poison identification scenario.
